name: sf-interview
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /ren:interview. Covers fresh-bootstrap, refresh on existing identity, emoji-free style respect, and handle validation.

tests.expected_output_summary: All 18 questions asked; identity.md written with full frontmatter.
binary_assertions: identity.md exists at ~/.startup-framework/wiki/identity.md after the run

identity.md frontmatter contains all required fields: title, type, schema_version, framework_version, handle, name, created, updated, phase, languages, working_style, communication_style, plans_before_code, tdd_attitude, strong_skills, growth_areas
handle matches the regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$
phase value is one of: ideation, building, shipping, other
All 18 question IDs are accounted for: either values are present OR the ID appears in skipped_questions
identity.md file size is less than 5120 bytes
The markdown body has the 5 required H2 sections (Background, Working style, Tech preferences, Strong opinions, What I contribute)

tests.expected_output_summary: Re-run detects existing identity.md, summarizes, offers full/sections/fields/cancel branch.
binary_assertions: Skill output mentions 'existing identity' OR shows the current-state summary
Skill offers at least 4 branch options matching: full, sections, fields, cancel
If branch=cancel, identity.md modification time is NOT updated
If branch=full and friend approves the diff, updated date is rewritten to today and other fields reflect new answers
If branch=full and friend declines the diff, NO file changes happen (no write)

tests.expected_output_summary: Friend chooses emoji-free communication_style; resulting identity.md body contains no emoji characters.
binary_assertions: Frontmatter communication_style value contains the substring 'no-emoji'
Markdown body contains zero Unicode characters in the Emoticon, Pictographs, Transport, or Misc-Symbols code blocks
Skill output / progress messages to the friend during the interview also contain zero emoji characters

tests.expected_output_summary: Non-kebab-case handle is rejected and re-prompted.
binary_assertions: If friend types 'Hazar Söz' as handle, skill rejects and re-prompts
If friend types 'hazar_soz' (underscore), skill rejects and re-prompts
If friend types '2hazar' (leading digit), skill rejects and re-prompts
If friend types 'hazar' (valid), skill accepts
The rejection message explains the kebab-case constraint explicitly

tests.expected_output_summary: Friend skips Q4 + Q16; identity.md records skipped question IDs.
binary_assertions: skipped_questions list contains 'q4-strong-skills' and 'q16-avoid-patterns'
strong_skills field has its default value (empty list) since Q4 was skipped
Markdown body's 'Strong opinions + non-goals' section is present but only contains the TDD framing from Q15 (no Q16 content)
Re-running /ren:interview detects skipped questions and offers to ask them again as part of a refresh

notes: Tests with trigger_test=true also verify the SKILL.md description correctly activates on the prompt (per ADR-012 Layer 1).
All assertions are binary. Subjective quality (e.g. 'is the interview pleasant?') is out of scope for eval.json per ADR-011.
